Built for a busy board: dispatch a prompt without first picking a session, slice the list down with real filters, sort and batch-act on what's left, and jump straight to whatever needs you. Plus a snappier list under load and a fistful of correctness fixes.

## Highlights

- **Smart dispatch.** Type a prompt from the board and Atrium routes it for you — opening a pre-filled new-session form, or sending it to the session that already fits — so you can fire off work without navigating to pick a target first.
- **Multi-select for batch actions.** Enter multi-select mode, mark a subset of sessions, and run a lifecycle action (pause, resume, kill, …) on just those — instead of acting on everything in view or one row at a time.
- **Advanced filter predicates.** Filter the board by structured predicates — `status`, `dirty` (uncommitted work), `behind` (base has moved on), and `pr` — to cut a large board down to exactly what needs attention.
- **Configurable within-group sort.** Choose how sessions order inside a group — by creation time or by status — so the most actionable ones surface where you expect them.
- **Jump to the next session that needs you.** One keystroke jumps straight to the next unread / NeedsInput session, instead of hunting down the list for the one waiting on you.
- **New sessions start from the freshest remote tip.** Atrium fetches before creating the worktree, so a new agent branches off current `main` rather than a stale local ref.
- **The new-session form remembers your draft.** Escape out of it and your title, prompt, and options are still there when you reopen — no retyping.

## Fixes

- **Responsive list under load.** The metadata poll fan-out is now throttled, so a board with many sessions stays smooth instead of stuttering as the count grows.
- **Instant detach.** Detaching no longer blocks on draining the session's stdout pump — it returns immediately.
- **Clipboard fixed.** Copying inside a tmux session now reaches your OS clipboard and stops clobbering the system clipboard's existing contents.
- **Failed prompt delivery is surfaced.** A queued prompt that fails to deliver now reports the failure instead of silently swallowing it.
- **Robust pause.** A session is marked Paused even when the post-remove worktree prune fails, so a partial teardown can't strand it in the wrong state.
- **Valid branch names for any title.** Non-ASCII or punctuation-only session titles now get a unique, valid branch slug instead of colliding or failing.
- **Claude detection upkeep.** The Claude trust gate is repaired for reworded prompts and its verified-version ceiling bumped; drift warnings are coarsened to minor versions so routine patch bumps stop nagging.

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZviBaratz/atrium/main/install.sh | bash
```

Or with Go — note this builds from source and reports a `dev` version (the
script installs the version-stamped, self-updating release binary):

```bash
go install github.com/ZviBaratz/atrium@v0.8.0
```

This is a 0.x release — APIs and behavior may change.
